justification: All pass criteria met: (1) All three ACs present with exact values — $47.80→47, $0.99→0, 12-month expiry all explicitly referenced. (2) Both domain rules present — 1pt/dollar with floor rounding and 12-month inactivity expiry. (3) Both architecture functions referenced — earnPoints(customerId, purchaseAmount) and checkExpiry(customerId) with Math.floor and loyalty.ts file location. (4) 365-day vs 12-month terminology flagged extensively with leap-year and month-boundary analysis, exceeding the rubric requirement to merely flag it. (5) No invented information — suggestions like date-fns/Temporal are implementation recommendations, not fabricated domain rules; assumptions are explicitly labeled as such.

observations.text: Terminology alignment analysis is exceptionally thorough — goes beyond flagging to provide concrete divergence examples (Jan 31 boundary case, leap years) and three resolution options with a recommendation
Correctly identifies Bird as domain authority and recommends MJ revise architecture rather than accepting the approximation
AC2 ($0.99→0 floor edge case) explicitly preserved — a key failure mode the rubric watches for
Low-confidence areas identify genuine gaps (checkExpiry return type, destructive vs state mutation) that neither source agent specified

Output structure uses escalations+confidence format rather than the expected handoff_brief with recipient/task_context/domain_rules/architecture_guidance/acceptance_criteria sections — content is present but scattered across a different schema
No explicit task_context summary of the loyalty points feature as a coherent paragraph — the context is distributed across escalation descriptions and confidence areas

grader_results.detail: field handoff_brief.domain_rules not found in JSON
